summary: Recent updates to WebGPU, a technology that enables advanced graphics and computation in web applications, enhance its robustness and manageability. Now, when users attempt to access WebGPU via `canvas.getContext("webgpu")` on unsupported platforms, it will return null—this helps prevent confusion for developers and users alike. Additionally, WebGPU has been disabled on 32-bit systems to ensure optimal performance and security, as the technology is better suited for 64-bit architectures. These changes contribute to a more reliable experience for developers working with graphics and computation in a browser environment, ensuring that they can target features only where they're fully supported. _Overall, users will notice **more consistent behavior** when using WebGPU on capable devices._
